role: Senior Data Analyst & Analytics Engineer - Franchise

evidence.domain_risk: Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
evidence.data_team_maturity: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks
evidence.jd_authorship: Code complex business logic (royalties, taxable turnover, margins)
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
evidence.autonomy_level: Support franchise partners' decisions regarding stock, supply chain, and pricing
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks; Design and maintain dashboards; Adapt existing dashboards from other business domains
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Building new data marts and dashboards is greenfield work, while adapting existing dashboards from other domains is fix/scale, together supporting the mixed classification.
evidence.ai_role: Build data marts and business layers using dbt on Databricks
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD describes standard analytics engineering work with dbt and Databricks and contains no mention of AI tool usage or AI-consuming infrastructure.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: The JD covers dashboarding, data modeling, and business logic but never references testing, data quality ownership, or QA tooling.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Row-level security and support for franchise partners' operational decisions signal operational trust and access-control concerns secondary to delivery, without explicit regulatory or audit framing.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD emphasizes governance and control (row-level security, documentation of KPIs and business rules) with no corresponding velocity signals like 'fast-paced' or 'rapid delivery' in the responsibilities section.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors in royalty calculations, taxable turnover, and margin logic directly affect partner financial reporting and tax compliance, making this financial data work with immediate consequences.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named tools in responsibilities with specific application context (building data marts), combined with the ability to adapt existing dashboards, indicates an established data infrastructure at mid-stage growth rather than early-stage or mature.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Technical specificity throughout (named tools, specific business logic, precise SQL/Tableau skills) indicates hiring manager who understands the technical work required.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The primary responsibility is serving franchise partners' operational and commercial decisions, making external franchisees the dominant stakeholder audience.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: While the role owns technical implementation (build, design, implement), the overarching framing emphasizes supporting franchise partner priorities rather than setting independent analytics strategy.
